1.  **Covariance Matrix Adaptation:** Unlike Differential Evolution (which operates on axes or random differences)
column2: CMA-ES learns the correlation between variables. It creates a ""search ellipse"" that aligns with the topology of the function (e.g.

column1: 2.  **Restart Mechanism (IPO):** The algorithm detects convergence (stagnation). When it gets stuck
column2: it restarts with a **doubled population size**. This theoretically guarantees finding the global optimum on multimodal functions given sufficient time

column1: 3.  **Step-Size Control:** It adapts a global step size ($\sigma$) using cumulative path length control
column2: allowing it to converge rapidly when close to the solution and expand search when far away.
